zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh St Philibert於大約1270年出生在英國素福爾可傑爾斯沃斯時，他的父親Hugh de St Philibert42歲，母親Euphemia Sulham36歲。他和Alice Blakenham至少有2個兒子。他去世於1304年12月31日在英國波克夏素利哈姆享年35歲。

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Southern French: variant of   St  .   Romain  .

variants.name: Romain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Alain, Altagrace, Aurore, Camille, Dominique, Edele, Georges, Henri, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Jean-Michel, Jeanmarie.
French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and English: variant of   Roman  .

nameMeanings.name: Philibert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Fernand, Mirielle, Pascale, Pierrot.

French: from the personal name Philibert, from ancient Germanic Filuberht, composed of the elements fila ‘much, very’ + berht ‘bright’, which was borne by a 7th-century Christian saint, abbot of Jumièges. The spelling has been influenced by personal names of Greek origin based on philein ‘to love’, e.g. Philip .

variants.name: Philbert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Monique.  German Kurt.
French and German (Bavaria): from the personal name Philbert, from ancient Germanic Filuberht (see Philibert ).

variants.name: Philip
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Varughese, Mathai, Babu, Reji, Anil, Binu, Neena, Prema, Raju, Reena, Susheela, Aleykutty.

Scottish, English, German, Dutch, and Jewish: from the Greek personal name Philippos (from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’). In the New Testament this name is borne by one of the apostles; it was also borne by various other early Christian saints. It owes part of its popularity to the medieval romances about Alexander the Great, whose father was Philip of Macedon. As a Jewish name, it represents a borrowing of the personal name from Christians. As a Highland Scottish surname, it represents an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhilib ‘son of Philip’. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Spanish Felipe , Catalan Felip, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Slovenian or Croatian Filip , Albanian Filipi , and their derivatives (see examples at Philips ). The name Philip is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Philipose and Pothen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Phillip .
French (southern) and Breton (Finistère): variant of   Philippe  .

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh St Philibert was born about 1270, in Chelsworth, Suffolk, England, his father, Hugh de St Philibert, was 42 and his mother, Euphemia Sulham, was 36. He had at least 2 sons with Alice Blakenham. He died on 31 December 1304, in Sulham, Berkshire, England, at the age of 35.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh St Philibert est né environ 1270, à Chelsworth, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Hugh de St Philibert, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Euphemia Sulham, avait 36 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Alice Blakenham. Il est décédé le 31 décembre 1304, à Sulham, Berkshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 35 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh St Philibertが約1270年にイギリススフォルクチェルスウオルスで生まれた時，父Hugh de St Philibertは42歳，母Euphemia Sulhamは36歳であった。彼はAlice Blakenhamとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1304年12月31日にイギリスバークシュアイアスルハムで35歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh St Philibert(이)가 대략 1270년 영국 수폴크 첼스워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh de St Philibert(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Euphemia Sulham(은)는 36세였다. 그는 Alice Blakenham(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1304년 12월 31일 영국 버크실르 설함에서 35세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh St Philibert родился около 1270 г. в местности челсворт, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Hugh de St Philibert было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Euphemia Sulham, 36. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Alice Blakenham. Он умер 31 декабря 1304 г. в местности сулхам, биркшир, Англия в возрасте 35 лет.
